author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: Writings in Connection with the Manichæan Controversy.
Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
Sin Not From God, But From The Will of Those Sinning.

ccel.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

title: Sin Not From God, But From The Will of Those Sinning.

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: But when we hear: "All things are from Him, and through Him, and in Him," we ought assuredly to understand all natures which naturally exist.

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: For sins, which do not preserve but vitiate nature, are not from Him; which sins, Holy Scripture in many ways testifies, are from the will of those sinning, especially in the passage where the apostle says: "But dost thou suppose this, O man, that judgest those who do such things, and doest them, that thou shall escape the judgment of God?

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: Or dost thou despise the riches of His goodness, and patience, and long-suffering, not knowing that the patience of God leadeth thee to repentance?

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: But according to the hardness of thy heart and thy impenitent heart, thou treasurest up for thyself wrath against the day of wrath and of the revelation of the just judgment of God, who will render unto every one according to his works."
